title: [RFE] when logging with journald driver, also log container name
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

I'm using `podman run --log-driver journald ...` but once the container if gone, it is hard for me to find its log messages as there is no container name field in journald log message:

```
# journalctl -f -o json
{ "__CURSOR" : "s=f263d4a0d98d4a948a83dc931b554356;i=1cbd;b=6f5ed51fdfb74e9cb250dc01b5f1443b;m=28e05e43a;t=5d0b423709df3;x=f375d94a76c634f5", "__REALTIME_TIMESTAMP" : "1636846990826995", "__MONOTONIC_TIMESTAMP" : "10972685370", "_BOOT_ID" : "6f5ed51fdfb74e9cb250dc01b5f1443b", "PRIORITY" : "3", "CODE_FILE" : "src/ctr_logging.c", "CODE_LINE" : "222", "CODE_FUNC" : "write_journald", "SYSLOG_IDENTIFIER" : "conmon", "_TRANSPORT" : "journal", "_UID" : "0", "_GID" : "0", "_COMM" : "conmon", "_EXE" : "/usr/bin/conmon", "_CAP_EFFECTIVE" : "1fffffffff", "_AUDIT_SESSION" : "20", "_AUDIT_LOGINUID" : "1000", "_SYSTEMD_SLICE" : "machine.slice", "_SELINUX_CONTEXT" : "unconfined_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0", "_MACHINE_ID" : "0837ed3ef6e74935996e727e185487c7", "_HOSTNAME" : "localhost.localdomain", "CONTAINER_ID_FULL" : "96a0ce71bb2ba975eb060dbcc73ec198116f7b16020a1077cf26871bfadb7bb8", "CONTAINER_ID" : "96a0ce71bb2b", "_PID" : "3135", "_CMDLINE" : "/usr/bin/conmon --api-version 1 -s -c 96a0ce71bb2ba975eb060dbcc73ec198116f7b16020a1077cf26871bfadb7bb8 -u 96a0ce71bb2ba975eb060dbcc73ec198116f7b16020a1077cf26871bfadb7bb8 -r /usr/bin/runc -b /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/96a0ce71bb2ba975eb060dbcc73ec198116f7b16020a1077cf26871bfadb7bb8/userdata -p /var/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/96a0ce71bb2ba975eb060dbcc73ec198116f7b16020a1077cf26871bfadb7bb8/userdata/pidfile -l journald --exit-dir /var/run/libpod/exits --socket-dir-path /var/run/libpod/socket --log-level error --runtime-arg --log-format=json --runtime-arg --log --runtime-arg=/var/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/96a0ce71bb2ba975eb060dbcc73ec198116f7b16020a1077cf26871bfadb7bb8/userdata/oci-log --conmon-pidfile /var/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/96a0ce71bb2ba975eb060dbcc73ec198116f7b16020a1077cf26871bfadb7bb8/userdata/conmon.pid --exit-command /usr/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /var/lib/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runroot --exit-command-arg /var/run/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg error --exit-command-arg --cgroup-manager --exit-command-arg systemd --exit-command-arg --tmpdir --exit-command-arg /var/run/libpod --exit-command-arg --runtime --exit-command-arg runc --exit-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg overlay --exit-command-arg --events-backend --exit-command-arg journald --exit-command-arg container --exit-command-arg cleanup --exit-command-arg --rm --exit-command-arg 96a0ce71bb2ba975eb060dbcc73ec198116f7b16020a1077cf26871bfadb7bb8", "_SYSTEMD_CGROUP" : "/machine.slice/libpod-conmon-96a0ce71bb2ba975eb060dbcc73ec198116f7b16020a1077cf26871bfadb7bb8.scope", "_SYSTEMD_UNIT" : "libpod-conmon-96a0ce71bb2ba975eb060dbcc73ec198116f7b16020a1077cf26871bfadb7bb8.scope", "MESSAGE" : "[2021-11-13 23:43:10 +0000] [7] [DEBUG] Closing connection.\n", "_SOURCE_REALTIME_TIMESTAMP" : "1636846990826569" }
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman run --name my-gunicorn-container --log-driver journald ...

2. journalctl -f -o json


**Describe the results you received:**

There is no container name in journald log message, only `CONTAINER_ID`, but that is not that handy when container is gone.


**Describe the results you expected:**

Would it be possible to add something like `"CONTAINER_NAME" : "my-gunicorn-container"` to the log message when logging like this?


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.12
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  Distribution:

    version: "7"
  MemFree: 746909696
  MemTotal: 1927004160
  OCIRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.0.0-69.rc10.el7_9.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 2147479552
  SwapTotal: 2147479552
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 2
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: localhost.localdomain
  kernel: 3.10.0-1160.42.2.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 3h 17m 45.51s (Approximately 0.12 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.redhat.io

  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 7
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: {}
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 9
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.6.4-29.el7_9.x86_64
```


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

No

comments.body: This is already the case, please update to a newer version.
